contract_summary: This is a put-wrapper that destroys the btrfs extent_state_cache. It calls btrfs_extent_state_leak_debug_check() as a debug preparation, then calls kmem_cache_destroy() to release the reference on the cache. After calling this function, the cache pointer must not be used again. This is typically called during module cleanup to finalize the cache lifecycle. There is no return value, and the operation is unconditional; the caller must ensure it is called exactly once during teardown, and that no other references to the cache remain.
